questions.q: Who was Ahalya in the Ramayana?
options: The wife of the sage Gautama
questions.explanation: Ahalya was the wife of the great sage Gautama Maharishi.

questions.q: Whose touch liberated Ahalya from her curse?
questions.explanation: Rama's touch (or the dust of his feet) freed Ahalya from the curse.

questions.q: On the way to which city did the liberation of Ahalya take place?
questions.explanation: Rama and Lakshmana, with Vishvamitra, were traveling to Mithila when they passed Gautama's hermitage.

questions.q: Into what form had Ahalya been transformed by the curse?
questions.explanation: By tradition the curse left Ahalya like a stone, motionless and unseen until Rama freed her.

questions.q: Which sage was guiding Rama and Lakshmana when they reached Ahalya?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishvamitra led the princes to Gautama's ashram and told them Ahalya's story.

questions.q: What was the name of Ahalya's husband, the sage who cursed her?
questions.explanation: Sage Gautama, Ahalya's husband, pronounced the curse upon her.

questions.q: Who accompanied Rama on the journey when Ahalya was liberated?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana traveled with Rama and Vishvamitra during this episode.

questions.q: The liberation of Ahalya is seen as a sign of what quality of Rama?
questions.explanation: The episode is celebrated as a sign of Rama's redeeming, purifying grace.

questions.q: Which deity disguised himself to deceive Ahalya, leading to the curse?
questions.explanation: Indra, king of the gods, disguised himself as Gautama to approach Ahalya.

questions.q: After her liberation, Ahalya was reunited with whom?
questions.explanation: Freed from the curse, Ahalya was reunited with her husband, the sage Gautama.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, where was Rama headed with Vishvamitra immediately after this episode?
options: To Janaka's court to see Shiva's bow
questions.explanation: They were traveling to Mithila for Janaka's sacrifice, where Rama would later lift and break Shiva's bow.

questions.q: How did Indra disguise himself to deceive Ahalya?
options: He took the form of the sage Gautama
He took the form of Rama
questions.explanation: Indra assumed the appearance of Gautama while the real sage was away, to deceive Ahalya.

questions.q: What was Ahalya famed for, making her a target of Indra's desire?
questions.explanation: Ahalya was renowned for her exceptional beauty, which drew Indra's attention.

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, what curse did Gautama also pronounce upon Indra?
options: That he would lose his testicles
That he would lose his throne forever
That he would turn to stone
questions.explanation: Gautama cursed Indra to lose his testicles, later replaced with those of a ram by the gods.

questions.q: According to Valmiki's Ramayana, how long was Ahalya to remain in her cursed state before deliverance?
options: Until Rama arrived at the hermitage
questions.explanation: Gautama decreed she would be released only when Rama came to the deserted ashram.

questions.q: During her penance awaiting liberation, what did Ahalya subsist on, by tradition?
options: Air alone, invisible to all beings
The fish of a nearby river
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Ahalya lay in penance subsisting on air, unseen by any creature, until Rama freed her.

questions.q: What did the sage Gautama do after pronouncing the curse on Ahalya?
options: He left the hermitage to perform austerities
He drowned himself in a river
He went to wage war on Indra
questions.explanation: Gautama abandoned the ashram and withdrew to the Himalayas for severe penance.

questions.q: In whose ashram did the liberation of Ahalya occur?
questions.explanation: The event took place at Gautama's own hermitage, which lay deserted after the curse.

questions.q: What did Rama and Lakshmana do upon meeting the liberated Ahalya?
options: They bowed and touched her feet in reverence
They asked her for a boon
They challenged her to a contest
questions.explanation: The princes paid their respects, honoring the restored Ahalya.

questions.q: Who returned to the hermitage to formally accept Ahalya after her liberation?
questions.explanation: Gautama returned and received Ahalya, their union restored after Rama's grace.

questions.q: How did the gods receive Ahalya's restoration, according to tradition?
options: They rejoiced and showered celestial flowers
They demanded Indra's punishment be lifted
questions.explanation: The episode is described with celestial rejoicing as Ahalya regained her form and honor.

questions.q: Ahalya is counted among which famous group in Hindu tradition?
options: The Panchakanya, the five exalted women
questions.explanation: Ahalya is one of the Panchakanya, five revered women remembered in a daily prayer.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, who is said to have created Ahalya, and from what?
options: Brahma, fashioning her as the most beautiful of women
questions.explanation: Valmiki states Brahma created Ahalya as a being of flawless beauty, assembling the finest features.

questions.q: Why, in the Valmiki tradition, did Brahma give Ahalya in marriage to Gautama rather than to a god?
options: To reward Gautama's restraint after he guarded her and circled the earth
Because Gautama won a contest of arms
questions.explanation: Gautama earned Ahalya by his self-control after Brahma entrusted her to him and he completed a circuit of the worlds.

questions.q: By tradition, which signal did Indra use to time his deception of Ahalya?
options: The crowing of a cock before dawn to mimic Gautama's pre-dawn departure
A messenger sent by the Ashvins
The blowing of a conch at dusk
questions.explanation: Indra is said to have used a cock's crow to make Gautama leave early for his ablutions, then approached Ahalya.

questions.q: What replacement did the gods provide for Indra after Gautama's emasculating curse?
questions.explanation: The gods, with the Pitris, fitted Indra with a ram's testicles, which is why rams were later offered in sacrifice.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, what does Gautama declare will purify Ahalya when Rama arrives?
options: Her hospitable reception of Rama at the ashram
The recitation of the Vedas by Vishvamitra
questions.explanation: Gautama said her penance would end and she would be purified when she honored Rama as a guest at the hermitage.

questions.q: The name 'Ahalya' is often traditionally interpreted to mean what?
options: One who is not ploughed or has no ugliness, hence flawless
questions.explanation: A common etymology reads 'a-halya' as without flaw or unploughed, fitting Brahma's flawless creation.

questions.q: Which son of Gautama and Ahalya is prominently known in tradition?
options: Shatananda, who served as priest in Janaka's court
questions.explanation: Shatananda, the son of Gautama and Ahalya, was the chief priest at King Janaka's court in Mithila.

questions.q: In Valmiki's text, what was Rama's reaction on learning Ahalya's full story from Vishvamitra?
options: He entered the hermitage to grant her deliverance as instructed
He demanded Indra be brought to justice first
questions.explanation: Following Vishvamitra's guidance, Rama entered Gautama's ashram and freed Ahalya, restoring her.
